step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows a serverless CI/CD manual approval workflow integrated with Slack (ChatOps). First, AWS CodePipeline hits the manual approval stage, triggering an SNS notification. SNS triggers a Lambda function that formats and sends an interactive Yes/No message to a Slack channel. DevOps agents click a button in Slack, which calls an API Gateway endpoint. API Gateway invokes another Lambda function to process the action and send the approval status back to CodePipeline, resuming the build to deploy to production.

graph.name: OpenFit ChatOps CI CD Pipeline with Slack
graph.notes: Serverless ChatOps deployment pipeline using Slack interactive buttons to approve production releases.

nodes.name: CodePipeline
nodes.notes: Orchestrates build, QA deploy, manual approval, and production deployment.

nodes.name: SNS Topic
nodes.notes: Receives approval state trigger from CodePipeline.

nodes.name: Notification Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggered by SNS to deliver an interactive Yes/No message to Slack.

nodes.name: Slack
nodes.notes: Collaboration tool where the team receives notifications and approves builds.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Receives webhook callbacks from interactive Slack buttons.

nodes.name: Approval Response Lambda
nodes.notes: Validates credentials and signals CodePipeline to resume deployment.
